summary: Ukrainian forces are testing two humanoid Phantom MK-1 robots from Foundation, signaling a new phase in battlefield robotics. Russian forces are adapting by using log-periodic directional antennas on "Molniyas" drones, operating on 3.3 GHz with vertical/horizontal polarization to resist jamming and evade interception. Ukraine is deploying fiber-optic FPV drones with up to 50 km range, immune to jamming, to strike deep into Russian rear areas, while also shifting to non-standard control frequencies. Russian logistics are reportedly collapsing under sustained Ukrainian drone attacks on supply lines, depots, and command centers, forcing Russia into a defensive posture. Ukrainian FPV drones are destroying artillery systems and tanks, with coordinated strikes combining artillery and drone attacks. Russian forces are countering with systems like "Yolka" to down Ukrainian fixed-wing "Hornet" UAVs, while also using FPV drones to eliminate Ukrainian personnel and aircraft, claiming 136 targets destroyed in a single day.
